name: student.work_authorization.guard
description: Keeps job, internship, and HR advice aware of student work-authorization and timing constraints without legal advice or unapproved outreach.

message.description: Job, internship, onboarding, or HR text involving student work authorization or timing.

examples: does this internship fit fall availability?
draft a work authorization clarification question
does this onboarding email need action?
is this full-time role risky for student constraints?
purpose: Guard career, internship, onboarding, and HR advice when F-1, CPT, OPT, STEM OPT, sponsorship, severe economic hardship, on-campus work, off-campus work, or start-date timing may affect what the user can safely say or do.
whenToUse: The request mentions F-1, CPT, OPT, STEM OPT, sponsorship, international student status, school authorization, on-campus work, off-campus work, or severe economic hardship.
The user asks how to answer work authorization, sponsorship, eligibility, start-date, or internship timing questions.
A job or internship listing may require unrestricted authorization, immediate start, full-time availability, or employment terms that could conflict with student constraints.
An HR or onboarding message asks for authorization details and the user needs cautious wording.
A deadline or start date may need Kairos awareness but should not create calendar events without approval.
whenNotToUse: Do not use for general job-fit screening with no work authorization, sponsorship, student status, or timing issue.
Do not use for employer Form I-9 or E-Verify procedure unless the request specifically includes student authorization concerns.
Do not use to give legal advice, guarantee eligibility, or replace school/international-office guidance.
Do not use to send emails, apply to roles, or update trackers without approval.
requiredContext: The user's actual current authorization status, if explicitly provided.
Whether the role is on-campus, off-campus, internship, co-op, full-time, part-time, remote, or volunteer.
The proposed start date, work hours, semester timing, and employer wording.
Whether the user has already checked with the school international office or DSO.
missingContextQuestions: Is this role on-campus or off-campus, and is it an internship, co-op, part-time role, or full-time role?
What start date, hours, and semester timing does the employer require?
What exact work authorization or sponsorship question are they asking?
Have you confirmed the relevant process with your school international office or DSO?

outputContract.format: Authorization-sensitive issue -> Known facts -> Missing facts -> Risk flags -> Safe wording -> School/official check -> Approval-gated draft if requested.
mustInclude: A clear note that this is not legal advice.
Missing facts that affect CPT, OPT, STEM OPT, on-campus, off-campus, or sponsorship wording.
A recommendation to verify with the school international office/DSO when eligibility is uncertain.
Cautious wording that preserves facts and avoids over-disclosure.
Approval-gated status for any outbound draft.
mustAvoid: Do not invent visa, CPT, OPT, STEM OPT, or sponsorship status.
Do not guarantee eligibility or authorize work.
Do not tell the user to misrepresent sponsorship or work authorization needs.
Do not send replies, create events, write files, or apply to jobs.
Do not expose private immigration details unless the user provided and requested them.

safetyRules: Do not provide legal advice or guarantee immigration/work eligibility.
Use only facts the user provided or confirmed memory; flag uncertainty.
Recommend official school/international-office or DSO checks when needed.
Keep HR, recruiter, and employer replies as approval-gated drafts.
Do not create calendar events, tasks, applications, or tracker updates without approval.
approvalRequiredFor: Sending employer, recruiter, school, or HR messages
Submitting applications or updating application trackers
Saving durable memory about authorization status
positiveExamples: My internship asks if I need sponsorship. How should I answer?
Does this fall internship fit F-1 CPT timing?
Draft a careful work authorization clarification question for a recruiter.
Can I work off-campus before OPT starts?
The offer starts before my school approval. What should I ask HR?
They asked about authorization on the application. What do I put?
negativeExamples: Score this GRC job based only on SOC 2 and NIST.
Make this text sound warmer with no visa or work topic.
Write a Windows login troubleshooting ticket.

evaluationPrompts.input: My internship asks if I need sponsorship. How should I answer?
Does this fall internship fit F-1 CPT timing?
evaluationPrompts.reason: Positive F-1 CPT and internship timing.

evaluationPrompts.input: Draft a careful work authorization clarification question for a recruiter.
evaluationPrompts.reason: Positive authorization wording and approval-gated draft.

evaluationPrompts.input: Can I work off-campus before OPT starts?
The offer starts before school authorization is approved. What should I ask HR?
evaluationPrompts.reason: Positive start-date and school authorization constraint.

evaluationPrompts.input: Score this SOC 2 compliance analyst role against Security+.
evaluationPrompts.reason: Negative GRC role screening without student authorization issue.

evaluationPrompts.input: Rewrite this lease email to sound nicer.
evaluationPrompts.reason: Negative writing request with no work authorization issue.

evaluationPrompts.input: Turn VPN not connecting into a help desk ticket note.
This internship has a weird start date. Is it okay?
evaluationPrompts.reason: Ambiguous internship timing; should ask for authorization and date details.

evaluationPrompts.input: They asked about authorization on the application. What do I put?
evaluationPrompts.reason: Ambiguous authorization wording; should ask for exact question and status.

evaluationPrompts.input: My internship asks if I need sponsorship and I want to apply anyway.
evaluationPrompts.reason: Multi-skill: authorization guard should pair with Job Application Ops.
